what: mux.feature has never parsed. The same EPARSE — inconsistent cell count within the table — reproduces on the CR's merge-base, so the defect predates this CR and was not introduced by it. An Examples table in the worktree placement-degradation outline dropped its branch column on two of three rows, leaving the whole file unreadable to any Gherkin tool. Repaired by restoring the dropped column; intent was unambiguous, since the surviving row and the outline's own Given fix both the branch and the three placements a binding cannot serve. The repair narrows nothing: the two rows previously expressed no readable case at all. File now parses at 65 scenarios, 0 errors.

cause: structural freeze verification could not run: the gherkin diff returned an all-zero summary because the BASE side failed to parse, not because nothing changed. An all-zero addOnly:true summary is indistinguishable from a clean additive diff at a glance, so the guard could have been read as passing while verifying nothing. Isolated the CR's own edit class instead by repairing a copy of the base, parsing both sides, and comparing scenario sets: 4 added, 0 removed, 0 modified — purely additive, freeze self-clears.

cold impl-judge found the added mux scenarios were phrased 'Given a caller running cyber-mux open ... Then the opened pane is REPORTED with the workspace', which is true only under a seam reading: a bare open --format json still emits the pane alone, so under a CLI reading the herdr case would fail while its tmux twin passed vacuously. An ambiguous frozen scenario is a defect even when the implementation is right. Reworded to assert what the adapter's open RETURNS, and recorded in both the scenario and the README that the workspace reaches a consumer through the layout manifest rather than a bare open. Implementation unchanged — the wording was wrong, not the code.
